title: Tangy Egyptian Rhubarb Tagine
description: A tangy and flavorful Egyptian-inspired dish featuring fresh rhubarb in a unique blend of spices and nuts, all slow-cooked in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot. This recipe is free from animal products, making it satvik-friendly.
instructions: Heat the ttukbaegi earthenware pot over low heat on your stovetop or in a conventional oven at 300°F (150°C).
In a blender or food processor, puree the dates, ginger, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, and salt until you get a smooth paste.
Add the tahini and lemon juice to the blender and blend until well combined.
Add the water to the blender and blend until you get a smooth, creamy sauce.
Add the sauce to the heated ttukbaegi pot and stir to combine.
Add the sliced rhubarb to the pot and stir to coat with the sauce.
Cover the pot and simmer over low heat for 20 minutes or until the rhubarb is tender.
While the rhubarb is cooking, toast the almonds in a small skillet over medium heat until fragrant and lightly browned.
Sprinkle the toasted almonds and sesame seeds over the rhubarb and sauce.
Serve warm or at room temperature, garnished with additional almonds and sesame seeds if desired.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
